hacker_news.title: Nissan Leaf drivers voice anger over app shutdown
hacker_news.author: hkt
hacker_news.summary: Carmaker’s decision to drop NissanConnect EV app on relatively recent cars fuels warnings from experts

linked_context.page_title: ‘Shockingly bad’: Nissan Leaf drivers voice anger over app shutdown
linked_context.page_description: Carmaker’s decision to drop NissanConnect EV app on relatively recent cars fuels warnings from experts
linked_context.page_excerpt:
Carmaker’s decision to drop NissanConnect EV app on relatively recent cars fuels warnings from experts

Owners of some Nissan Leaf electric vehicles are angry after the carmaker announced it would shut down an app that lets them remotely control battery charging and other functions.

Drivers of Leaf cars made before May 2019 and the e-NV200 van (produced until 2022) have been told that the NissanConnect EV app linked to their vehicles will “cease operation” from 30 March. This means they will lose remote services, including turning on the heating, and some map features.

hacker_news.content_context:
Carmaker’s decision to drop NissanConnect EV app on relatively recent cars fuels warnings from experts

Carmaker’s decision to drop NissanConnect EV app on relatively recent cars fuels warnings from experts Owners of some Nissan Leaf electric vehicles are angry after the carmaker announced it would shut down an app that lets them remotely control battery charging and other functions. Drivers of Leaf cars made before May 2019 and the e-NV200 van (produced until 2022) have been told that the NissanConnect EV app linked to their vehicles will “cease operation” from 30 March. This means they will lose remote services, including turning on the heating, and some map features.

hacker_news.title: Be Human
hacker_news.author: reidrac

linked_context.page_excerpt:
Recently, Hacker News updated the comments section of their guidelines :

Don’t post generated comments or AI-edited comments. HN is for conversation between humans.

And increasingly this is turning to be a key point in human interaction online, which also affects workplace dynamics. I’m not referring only to remote work, because in all the companies I have worked for in the last 20 years there has always been a digital component: some sort of internal instant messaging, email, code reviews, etc.

hacker_news.content_context:

hacker_news.title: M5 MacBook Air review: Still the best MacBook for almost everybody
hacker_news.author: y1n0
hacker_news.summary: The M5 MacBook Air is a minor upgrade, but minor upgrades add up over time.

linked_context.page_title: M5 MacBook Air review: Still the best MacBook for almost everybody
linked_context.page_description: The M5 MacBook Air is a minor upgrade, but minor upgrades add up over time.
linked_context.page_excerpt:
The M5 MacBook Air is a minor upgrade, but minor upgrades add up over time.

The M5 Pro and M5 Max in the new MacBook Pros are interesting not because they deliver a solid speed increase for Apple’s fastest laptop processors but because they also include substantial under-the-hood changes. And the MacBook Neo is interesting because, while the hardware has limits , it’s quite a capable and high-quality computer for its $599 starting price.

And then there’s the M5 MacBook Air, which was also released this week.

hacker_news.title: Next-forge 6 is now available
hacker_news.author: y1n0
hacker_news.summary: next-forge v6 comes with a number of DX improvements, an agent skill, and new guides for quickstart, Docker, and migration paths.

linked_context.page_description: next-forge v6 comes with a number of DX improvements, an agent skill, and new guides for quickstart, Docker, and migration paths.
linked_context.page_excerpt:
next-forge is a production-grade Turborepo template for Next.js apps, designed to be a comprehensive, opinionated starting point for new apps.

This major release comes with a number of DX improvements, an agent skill, and new guides for quickstart, Docker, and migration paths.

You can now install a next-forge skill into your preferred agent, giving it structured knowledge of next-forge architecture, packages, and common tasks.

You can now install a next-forge skill into your preferred agent, giving it structured knowledge of next-forge architecture, packages, and common tasks.

hacker_news.title: RAM is getting expensive, so squeeze the most from it
hacker_news.author: rbanffy
hacker_news.summary: : Zram versus zswap – two ways to get a quart into a pint pot

linked_context.page_title: RAM is getting expensive, so squeeze the most from it
linked_context.page_description: : Zram versus zswap – two ways to get a quart into a pint pot
linked_context.page_excerpt:
Linux has two ways to do memory compression – zram and zswap – but you rarely hear about the second. The Register compares and contrasts them.

The Linux kernel's zram tool has been in the news this week. Phoronix reports that the new patch could make some zram operations 50 percent faster.

Thanks to the increasingly desperate flailing by the hucksters selling automatic plagiarism machines, RAM has got much more expensive and the price rises are expected to continue . Apple has even quietly dropped the 512 GB RAM model of its Mac Studio. This is not a good time to buy computers with lots of RAM, or to add more RAM to existing machines.

hacker_news.content_context:
: Zram versus zswap – two ways to get a quart into a pint pot

Linux has two ways to do memory compression – zram and zswap – but you rarely hear about the second. The Register compares and contrasts them. The Linux kernel's zram tool has been in the news this week. Phoronix reports that the new patch could make some zram operations 50 percent faster. Thanks to the increasingly desperate flailing by the hucksters selling automatic plagiarism machines, RAM has got much more expensive and the price rises are expected to continue . Apple has even quietly dropped the 512 GB RAM model of its Mac Studio. This is not a good time to buy computers with lots of RAM, or to add more RAM to existing machines.

hacker_news.title: Before the bio, there was the forum signature
hacker_news.author: carlos-menezes
hacker_news.summary:
In 2008, I got my first laptop. I was obsessed with GTA San Andreas and wanted to play it online, so I downloaded the multiplayer client. Nobody made me register on the forums but it was the obvious next step. That's where the servers were listed, where mods were shared. To some extent, where part of the game lived. I was new to all of it and I spent equal parts time reading threads and playing...

"My gaming forum signatures from 15 or 16 years ago" , by Reddit user theskyguyuk .

That was the signature. A tiny patch of internet real estate, appended to every post you ever made, that said: this is who I am , or at least, this is how I want to be seen in this community .

top_trends.title: Nissan Leaf drivers voice anger over app shutdown
top_trends.author: hkt
top_trends.summary: Carmaker’s decision to drop NissanConnect EV app on relatively recent cars fuels warnings from experts

linked_context.page_title: ‘Shockingly bad’: Nissan Leaf drivers voice anger over app shutdown
linked_context.page_description: Carmaker’s decision to drop NissanConnect EV app on relatively recent cars fuels warnings from experts
linked_context.page_excerpt:
Carmaker’s decision to drop NissanConnect EV app on relatively recent cars fuels warnings from experts

Owners of some Nissan Leaf electric vehicles are angry after the carmaker announced it would shut down an app that lets them remotely control battery charging and other functions.

Drivers of Leaf cars made before May 2019 and the e-NV200 van (produced until 2022) have been told that the NissanConnect EV app linked to their vehicles will “cease operation” from 30 March. This means they will lose remote services, including turning on the heating, and some map features.

top_trends.content_context:
Carmaker’s decision to drop NissanConnect EV app on relatively recent cars fuels warnings from experts

Carmaker’s decision to drop NissanConnect EV app on relatively recent cars fuels warnings from experts Owners of some Nissan Leaf electric vehicles are angry after the carmaker announced it would shut down an app that lets them remotely control battery charging and other functions. Drivers of Leaf cars made before May 2019 and the e-NV200 van (produced until 2022) have been told that the NissanConnect EV app linked to their vehicles will “cease operation” from 30 March. This means they will lose remote services, including turning on the heating, and some map features.
